name: castxml
title: castxml
summary: C-family abstract syntax tree XML output tool
description:
C-family abstract syntax tree XML output tool
Parse C-family source files and optionally write a subset of the
Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) to a representation in XML.
This is the successor to gccxml.

commands.name: castxml
commands.description: Create an XML representation of C++ declarations

arguments.description: Run the static analyzer
arguments.raw: --analyze  Run the static analyzer

arguments.description: Static analyzer report output format
arguments.raw: --analyzer-output  Static analyzer report output format

arguments.description: Search $prefix$file for executables, libraries, and data files. If $prefix is a direc-
arguments.raw: -B  Search $prefix$file for executables, libraries, and data files. If $prefix is a direc-

arguments.description: Pass -b <arg> to the linker on AIX
arguments.raw: -b  Pass -b <arg> to the linker on AIX

arguments.description: Include comments in preprocessed output
arguments.raw: -C  Include comments in preprocessed output

arguments.description: Only run preprocess, compile, and assemble steps
arguments.raw: -c  Only run preprocess, compile, and assemble steps

arguments.description: Write gccxml-format output to <src>.xml or file named by '-o' Use this option if you
arguments.raw: --castxml-gccxml  Write gccxml-format output to <src>.xml or file named by '-o' Use this option if you

arguments.description: Include comments from within macros in preprocessed output
arguments.raw: -CC  Include comments from within macros in preprocessed output

arguments.description: OpenCL only. Allow denormals to be flushed to zero.
arguments.raw: -cl-denorms-are-zero  OpenCL only. Allow denormals to be flushed to zero.

arguments.raw: -cl-finite-math-only  OpenCL only. Allow floating-point optimizations that assume arguments and results are

arguments.description: OpenCL only. Generate kernel argument metadata.
arguments.raw: -cl-kernel-arg-info  OpenCL only. Generate kernel argument metadata.

arguments.description: OpenCL only. Allow use of less precise MAD computations in the generated binary.
arguments.raw: -cl-mad-enable  OpenCL only. Allow use of less precise MAD computations in the generated binary.

arguments.description: ros and -cl-mad-enable.
arguments.raw: -cl-no-signed-ze  ros and -cl-mad-enable.

arguments.description: OpenCL only. Allow use of less precise no signed zeros computations in the generated
arguments.raw: -cl-no-signed-zeros  OpenCL only. Allow use of less precise no signed zeros computations in the generated

arguments.description: only. Disables all standard includes containing non-native compiler types and
arguments.raw: -cl-no-stdinc  only. Disables all standard includes containing non-native compiler types and

arguments.description: OpenCL only. This option disables all optimizations. By default optimizations are en-
arguments.raw: -cl-opt-disable  OpenCL only. This option disables all optimizations. By default optimizations are en-

arguments.description: only. Treat double precision floating-point constant as single precision con-
arguments.raw: -cl-single-precision-constant  only. Treat double precision floating-point constant as single precision con-

arguments.description: OpenCL only. This option is added for compatibility with OpenCL 1.0.
arguments.raw: -cl-strict-aliasing  OpenCL only. This option is added for compatibility with OpenCL 1.0.

arguments.description: OpenCL only. Defines that the global work-size be a multiple of the work-group size
arguments.raw: -cl-uniform-work-group-size  OpenCL only. Defines that the global work-size be a multiple of the work-group size

arguments.description: OpenCL only. Allow unsafe floating-point optimizations. Also implies -cl-no-signed-ze-
arguments.raw: -cl-unsafe-math-optimizations  OpenCL only. Allow unsafe floating-point optimizations. Also implies -cl-no-signed-ze-

arguments.description: ClangIR: Disable MLIR module verifier
arguments.raw: -clangir-disable-verifier  ClangIR: Disable MLIR module verifier

arguments.description: CUDA code for both host and device (default). Has no effect on non-CUDA compi-
arguments.raw: --cuda-compile-host-device  CUDA code for both host and device (default). Has no effect on non-CUDA compi-

arguments.description: Compile CUDA code for device only
arguments.raw: --cuda-device-only  Compile CUDA code for device only

arguments.description: Compile CUDA code for host only. Has no effect on non-CUDA compilations.
arguments.raw: --cuda-host-only  Compile CUDA code for host only. Has no effect on non-CUDA compilations.

arguments.description: Enable device-side debug info generation. Disables ptxas optimizations.
arguments.raw: --cuda-noopt-device-debug  Enable device-side debug info generation. Disables ptxas optimizations.

arguments.description: Ignore environment variables to detect CUDA installation
arguments.raw: --cuda-path-ignore-env  Ignore environment variables to detect CUDA installation

arguments.description: Add directory to the C++ SYSTEM include search path
arguments.raw: -cxx-isystem  Add directory to the C++ SYSTEM include search path

arguments.description: win targets)
arguments.raw: -dar  win targets)

arguments.description: Generate code for an additional runtime variant of the deployment target
arguments.raw: -darwin-target-variant  Generate code for an additional runtime variant of the deployment target

arguments.description: Specify the darwin target variant triple
arguments.raw: -darwin-target-variant-triple  Specify the darwin target variant triple

arguments.description: Print macro definitions in -E mode in addition to normal output
arguments.raw: -dD  Print macro definitions in -E mode in addition to normal output

arguments.description: Filename (or -) to write dependency output to
arguments.raw: -dependency-file  Filename (or -) to write dependency output to

arguments.description: Print include directives in -E mode in addition to normal output
arguments.raw: -dI  Print include directives in -E mode in addition to normal output

arguments.description: Print macro definitions in -E mode instead of normal output
arguments.raw: -dM  Print macro definitions in -E mode instead of normal output

arguments.description: Directory to output dSYM's (if any) to
arguments.raw: -dsym-dir  Directory to output dSYM's (if any) to

arguments.description: Use <dumpfpx> as a prefix to form auxiliary and dump file names
arguments.raw: -dumpdir  Use <dumpfpx> as a prefix to form auxiliary and dump file names

arguments.description: Display the compiler's target processor
arguments.raw: -dumpmachine  Display the compiler's target processor

arguments.description: Display the version of the compiler
arguments.raw: -dumpversion  Display the version of the compiler

arguments.description: Only run the preprocessor
arguments.raw: -E  Only run the preprocessor

arguments.description: Emit Clang AST files for source inputs
arguments.raw: -emit-ast  Emit Clang AST files for source inputs

arguments.description: Build ASTs and then lower to ClangIR
arguments.raw: -emit-cir  Build ASTs and then lower to ClangIR

arguments.description: Generate additional symbol graphs for extended modules.
arguments.raw: --emit-extension-symbol-graphs  Generate additional symbol graphs for extended modules.

arguments.description: Generate Interface Stub Files.
arguments.raw: -emit-interface-stubs  Generate Interface Stub Files.

arguments.description: Use the LLVM representation for assembler and object files
arguments.raw: -emit-llvm  Use the LLVM representation for assembler and object files

arguments.description: Generate Interface Stub Files, emit merged text not binary.
arguments.raw: -emit-merged-ifs  Generate Interface Stub Files, emit merged text not binary.

arguments.description: Enable linker job to emit a static library.
arguments.raw: --emit-static-lib  Enable linker job to emit a static library.

arguments.description: Generate Extract API information as a side effect of compilation.
arguments.raw: -emit-symbol-graph  Generate Extract API information as a side effect of compilation.

arguments.description: Start emitting warnings for unused driver arguments
arguments.raw: --end-no-unused-arguments  Start emitting warnings for unused driver arguments

arguments.description: Extract API information
arguments.raw: -extract-api  Extract API information

arguments.description: Follows the AAPCS standard that all volatile bit-field write generates at least one
arguments.raw: -faapcs-bitfield-load  Follows the AAPCS standard that all volatile bit-field write generates at least one

arguments.description: Follow the AAPCS standard requirement stating that volatile bit-field width is dictated
arguments.raw: -faapcs-bitfield-width  Follow the AAPCS standard requirement stating that volatile bit-field width is dictated

arguments.description: Use hardened lowering for jump-table dispatch
arguments.raw: -faarch64-jump-table-hardening  Use hardened lowering for jump-table dispatch

arguments.description: Emit an address-significance table
arguments.raw: -faddrsig  Emit an address-significance table

arguments.description: Enable C++17 aligned allocation functions
arguments.raw: -faligned-allocation  Enable C++17 aligned allocation functions

arguments.description: Treat editor placeholders as valid source code
arguments.raw: -fallow-editor-placeholders  Treat editor placeholders as valid source code

arguments.description: Use ANSI escape codes for diagnostics
arguments.raw: -fansi-escape-codes  Use ANSI escape codes for diagnostics

arguments.description: Enable external API notes support
arguments.raw: -fapinotes  Enable external API notes support

arguments.description: Enable module-based external API notes support
arguments.raw: -fapinotes-modules  Enable module-based external API notes support

arguments.description: Use Apple's kernel extensions ABI
arguments.raw: -fapple-kext  Use Apple's kernel extensions ABI

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use castxml because C-family abstract syntax tree XML output tool.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use castxml as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `castxml ( <castxml-opt> | <clang-opt> | <src> )...` as documented upstream for castxml.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair castxml with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `castxml --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: castxml - Create an XML representation of C++ declarations
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install castxml. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `castxml ( <castxml-opt> | <clang-opt> | <src> )...`. C-family abstract syntax tree XML output tool.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: C-family abstract syntax tree XML output tool.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: castxml - Create an XML representation of C++ declarations.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install castxml
Install with: sudo apt install castxml
